# React Dynamic List

> A React component for managing dynamic lists with shared state, event-driven updates, and slot-based rendering.

## Installation

```bash
npm install @jswork/react-dynamic-list @jswork/react-list
```

## Features

- Global store: list data lives outside React, shared across components by name
- Event-driven: pub/sub system propagates changes to all subscribers
- Slot-based rendering: customize item and empty states via `slots`
- Constraints: `min`/`max` bounds with `canAdd`/`canRemove` flags
- Change tracking: every mutation emits a `ChangeEvent` with action, data, and index
- SSR-safe: works with server-side rendering (no `window` dependency)

## Components

### `<DynamicList>`

The main declarative component. Wraps `@jswork/react-list` with list management.

```tsx
import { DynamicList } from '@jswork/react-dynamic-list';
import type { ChangeEvent } from '@jswork/react-dynamic-list';
```

**Props:**

| Prop | Type | Required | Description |
|------|------|----------|-------------|
| `name` | `string` | Yes | Unique key for the shared store |
| `defaults` | `() => T` | Yes | Factory function for new items |
| `data` | `T[]` | No | Initial data (syncs on change) |
| `min` | `number` | No | Minimum list length |
| `max` | `number` | No | Maximum list length |
| `onChange` | `(event: ChangeEvent<T>) => void` | No | Callback on every mutation |
| `slots` | `{ item: ComponentType; empty?: ComponentType }` | Yes | Slot components for rendering |
| `keyExtractor` | `(item: T, index: number) => string \| number` | No | Key function (defaults to `INDEX`) |
| `...rest` | `ReactListProps<T>` | No | Any prop from `@jswork/react-list` |

**Usage:**

```tsx
interface Todo {
  id: string;
  title: string;
  done: boolean;
}

const defaults = (): Todo => ({ id: crypto.randomUUID(), title: '', done: false });

<DynamicList<Todo>
  name="todos"
  defaults={defaults}
  data={[{ id: '1', title: 'Hello', done: false }]}
  max={10}
  onChange={(e) => console.log(e.action, e.index, e.data)}
  slots={{
    item: ({ item, index }) => <div>{item.title}</div>,
    empty: () => <div>No items</div>
  }}
/>
```

### `useCommand`

Hook for imperative list access. Shares the same store as `<DynamicList>` by name.

```tsx
import { useCommand } from '@jswork/react-dynamic-list';
```

**Signature:**

```ts
function useCommand<T>(name: string, options?: ListOptions<T>): ListApi<T>
```

**Options (`ListOptions<T>`):**

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `defaults` | `() => T` | Yes | Factory function for new items |
| `min` | `number` | No | Minimum list length |
| `max` | `number` | No | Maximum list length |

**Return (`ListApi<T>`):**

Returns `{ state, actions }` — state is reactive data, actions are imperative methods.

| Path | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `state.list` | `T[]` | Current list data |
| `state.change` | `ChangeEvent<T> \| null` | Last change event |
| `state.canAdd` | `boolean` | `false` when list length >= `max` |
| `state.canRemove` | `boolean` | `false` when list length <= `min` |
| `actions.add` | `() => void` | Append item via `defaults()` |
| `actions.remove` | `(index: number) => void` | Remove item at index |
| `actions.update` | `(index: number, updater: (prev: T) => T) => void` | Update item at index |
| `actions.set` | `(items: T[]) => void` | Replace entire list |
| `actions.up` | `(index: number) => void` | Move item at index up (swap with previous) |
| `actions.down` | `(index: number) => void` | Move item at index down (swap with next) |

**Usage:**

```tsx
function MyControls() {
  const { state, actions } = useCommand<Item>(
    'todos',
    { max: 5, defaults: () => ({ id: crypto.randomUUID(), title: '', done: false }) }
  );
  const { list, canAdd, canRemove } = state;
  const { add, remove } = actions;

  return (
    <>
      <button disabled={!canAdd} onClick={add}>Add</button>
      <button disabled={!canRemove} onClick={() => remove(list.length - 1)}>Remove Last</button>
    </>
  );
}
```

## Types

```ts
type ListAction = 'add' | 'remove' | 'update' | 'set' | 'up' | 'down';

interface ChangeEvent<T> {
  action: ListAction;
  data: T[];       // full list after mutation
  index?: number;  // affected index (undefined for set)
}

interface ListState<T> {
  list: T[];
  change: ChangeEvent<T> | null;
  canAdd: boolean;
  canRemove: boolean;
}

interface ListActions<T> {
  add: () => void;
  remove: (index: number) => void;
  update: (index: number, updater: (prev: T) => T) => void;
  set: (items: T[]) => void;
  up: (index: number) => void;
  down: (index: number) => void;
}

interface ListApi<T> {
  state: ListState<T>;
  actions: ListActions<T>;
}
```

## Architecture

```
┌─────────────┐     ┌──────────┐     ┌──────────┐
│ DynamicList  │────▶│  Store   │◀────│useCommand│
│ (component)  │     │ (global) │     │  (hook)  │
└─────────────┘     └────┬─────┘     └──────────┘
                         │
                    ┌────▼─────┐
                    │  Event   │
                    │ (pub/sub)│
                    └──────────┘
```

- **Store** (internal): Module-scoped `Map<string, unknown[]>` holding list data outside React tree
- **Event** (internal): Name-keyed pub/sub for reactive cross-component propagation
- **useCommand**: Bridge between store/events and React state via `useState`, returns `{ state, actions }`
- **DynamicList**: Declarative wrapper combining `useCommand` + `@jswork/react-list`

All components using the same `name` share the same list data and react to each other's mutations.

## File Structure

```
packages/lib/src/
├── index.ts              # Barrel exports
├── types.ts             # ListAction, ChangeEvent, ListApi, ListState, ListActions
├── store.ts             # Global in-memory store (getList, setList, addToList, removeAt, updateAt)
├── event.ts             # Pub/sub system (subscribe, emitChange)
├── use-command.ts       # React hook (useCommand)
└── dynamic-list.tsx     # DynamicList component
```

## Notes for AI Agents

- `defaults` is required in both `<DynamicList>` and `useCommand` — it is a factory function `() => T`, not a value
- `name` is the shared-state key — all hooks/components with the same `name` see the same list
- `useCommand` returns `{ state, actions }` — state holds reactive data (`list`, `change`, `canAdd`, `canRemove`), actions hold imperative methods (`add`, `remove`, `update`, `set`, `up`, `down`)
- `ChangeEvent.data` contains the **full list after mutation**, not the delta
- `ChangeEvent.index` is `undefined` for `set` actions
- `keyExtractor` defaults to `INDEX` (index-based key) — use a custom extractor for stable keys when items reorder
- The store is global and lives for the module's lifetime — tests can import `setList` directly from `store.ts` to reset in `beforeEach`
- `min`/`max` are soft constraints enforced at the hook level — direct store calls bypass them
- Store and Event modules are internal — not exported from the public API. Tests import directly from source files
- This package depends on `@jswork/react-list` as a peer dependency for the rendering layer
